instruments: Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance (Cap. 615)
standing_summary: The dual licence arrangement for virtual asset trading platforms (VATPs) -- a Securities and Futures Ordinance licence for platforms trading at least one security token, and an Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance licence for all centralised VA exchanges -- has been in force since 1 June 2023. As of the SFC's most recently updated public list, 13 platforms are licensed. The SFC's ASPIRe roadmap, published 19 February 2025, continues to drive expansion: staking by licensed platforms has been permitted since 7 April 2025, and a 27 May 2026 circular set out how VATPs may offer HKMA-licensed stablecoins for trading.

SFC circular 26EC26: stablecoin services by VATPs (27 May 2026)

open_items: Further ASPIRe workstream initiatives are ongoing and not all have concluded.
Additional platform licensing continues incrementally (e.g. licences granted February and May 2026).
The separate virtual asset dealer, custodian, advisory, and management licensing regimes (a related but distinct pillar) remain at bill-pending stage, not yet in force.
